title.value: StepSearch: Igniting LLMs Search Ability via Step-Wise Proximal Policy Optimization
title.evidence: StepSearch: Igniting LLMs Search Ability via Step-Wise Proximal Policy Optimization

multihop_reasoning_challenge.value: Efficient multi-hop reasoning requires Large Language Models (LLMs) based agents to acquire high-value external knowledge iteratively.
multihop_reasoning_challenge.evidence: Efficient multi-hop reasoning requires Large Language Models (LLMs) based agents to acquire high-value external knowledge iteratively.

sparse_reward_problem.value: Existing RL-based search agents depend on coarse global rewards, lacking fine-grained supervision of intermediate queries and multi-step retrievals.
sparse_reward_problem.evidence: However, existing RL-based search agents depend on coarse global rewards, lacking fine-grained supervision of intermediate queries and multi-step retrievals—an approach inadequate for the dependencies inherent in complex multi-hop reasoning.

search_efficiency_problem.value: Most multi-hop QA frameworks omit explicit guidance on query trajectories, leaving a critical gap in search-path modeling.
search_efficiency_problem.evidence: Moreover, most multi-hop QA frameworks omit explicit guidance on query trajectories (e.g., intermediate search keywords or document usage), leaving a critical gap in search-path modeling.

existing_method_performance.value: Traditional methods suffer from intrinsic knowledge gaps and static, inefficient knowledge-assimilation mechanisms.
existing_method_performance.evidence: However, complex multi-hop QA still suffers from intrinsic knowledge gaps and static, inefficient knowledge-assimilation mechanisms.

framework_overview.value: We introduce StepSearch, a framework for search LLMs that trained with step-wise proximal policy optimization method.
framework_overview.evidence: To address this gap in existing research, we introduce StepSearch, a framework for search LLMs that trained with step-wise proximal policy optimization method.

subproblem_decomposition.value: We construct a multi-turn Q&A dataset with subquestion-level search trajectories.
subproblem_decomposition.evidence: In this pilot study, we construct a multi-turn Q&A dataset with subquestion-level search trajectories.

search_keyword_generation.value: Each enhanced step question is then reformulated into a set of N search queries to facilitate information retrieval.
search_keyword_generation.evidence: Each enhanced step question is then reformulated into a set of N search queries to facilitate information retrieval.

cross_engine_validation.value: Queries are issued to M sources (e.g., Google, Bing, Wiki-18), and only those returning valid results in at least ⌈M / 2⌉ sources are retained.
cross_engine_validation.evidence: Queries are issued to M sources (e.g., Google, Bing, Wiki-18), and only those returning valid results in at least ⌈M / 2⌉ sources are retained.

dataset_scale.value: We developed a novel MuSiQue-based pipeline, contributing 60k filtered sub-question search keywords.
dataset_scale.evidence: We develop a novel MuSiQue-based pipeline, contributing 60k filtered sub-question search keywords that generalize across retrieval datasets.

global_rewards.value: We augment PPO with token-level rewards—information gain and redundancy penalties—for both query formulation and document retrieval.
global_rewards.evidence: We augment PPO with token-level rewards—information gain and redundancy penalties—for both query formulation and document retrieval.

step_rewards.value: We introduce a search-key reward to promote informative query issuance directly.
step_rewards.evidence: In addition to the standard format and final-answer reward, we introduce a search-key reward to promote informative query issuance directly.

algorithm_basis.value: Our optimization algorithm combines the above-mentioned Search Steps supervision reward based on the currently widely used actor-critic approach PPO.
algorithm_basis.evidence: Our optimization algorithm combines the above-mentioned Search Steps supervision reward based on the currently widely used actor-critic approach PPO.

core_mechanism.value: We segment each turn into think, search, and answer phases and assign token-level rewards.
core_mechanism.evidence: Our method further diverges from vanilla PPO by segmenting each turn into think, search, and answer phases and assigning token-level rewards.

mathematical_formulation.value: We optimize the policy πθ with the reference policy πθref by maximizing a specific objective function.
mathematical_formulation.evidence: Then optimize the policy πθ with the reference policy πθref by maximizing the following objective.

performance_improvements.value: StepSearch outperforms standard RL baselines by 5.7%, 9.1%, and 15.2% absolutely on diverse multi-hop QA benchmarks.
performance_improvements.evidence: StepSearch outperforms standard RL baselines by 5.7%, 9.1%, and 15.2% absolutely on diverse multi-hop QA benchmarks.

data_efficiency.value: Our method demonstrates significant improvements using only 19k training data.
data_efficiency.evidence: demonstrating the effectiveness of fine-grained, stepwise supervision in optimizing deep search LLMs.

generalization_capability.value: StepSearch shows higher adaptability to out-of-domain knowledge bases.
generalization_capability.evidence: StepSearch shows higher adaptability to out-of-domain knowledge bases.

ablation_study_findings.value: The step-wise token-level reward mechanism has more obvious advantages and stability compared to the global reward of search process.
ablation_study_findings.evidence: The step-wise token-level reward mechanism has more obvious advantages and stability compared to the global reward of search process.

key_innovations.value: We introduce a dual reward mechanism that combines global and step-wise rewards.
key_innovations.evidence: We introduce a dual reward mechanism that combines global and step-wise rewards.

method_comparison.value: StepSearch consistently outperforms strong baseline Search-RL methods.
method_comparison.evidence: StepSearch consistently outperforms strong baseline Search-RL methods.

case_study_example.value: StepSearch shows the effectiveness of process supervision in various datasets.
case_study_example.evidence: The success of various methods has further proved the effectiveness of process supervision.

current_limitations.value: Evaluation has been restricted to text-only question answering.
current_limitations.evidence: Despite the advances demonstrated by our retrieval-augmented reasoning framework, it remains subject to several important limitations.

future_research_directions.value: We plan to enhance the agent’s post-retrieval comprehension and extend our framework beyond text-only QA.
future_research_directions.evidence: Looking ahead, we plan to enhance the agent’s post-retrieval comprehension and extend our framework beyond text-only QA.

key_contribution.value: The core contribution lies in the StepSearch framework that integrates iterative retrieval with explicit step-wise supervision.
key_contribution.evidence: In general, our core contribution lies in the StepSearch framework that integrates iterative retrieval with explicit step-wise supervision.

broader_implications.value: This research impacts the development of AI systems by improving multi-hop reasoning capabilities.
broader_implications.evidence: This research impacts the development of AI systems by improving multi-hop reasoning capabilities.
